name: Walsall
description: Walsall is the principal town in the Metropolitan Borough of Walsall in the West Midlands. With a mix of Victorian terraces, 1960s–70s semi-detached estates, and modern new-builds, property maintenance needs are varied and year-round.
commonRepairNeeds: fence repairs after West Midlands winters
gutter clearing due to heavy rainfall
lock changes on older terraced properties
seasonalIssues: winter gutter blockages from leaf fall

name: Bloxwich
description: Bloxwich is a large suburban district in the north of Walsall, known for its mix of traditional terraces and post-war council housing. The area has a strong community feel with high demand for affordable property maintenance.
propertyTypes: some Victorian terraced housing near the high street
commonRepairNeeds: interior painting of dated council-style interiors
flat-pack furniture assembly for downsizing families
seasonalIssues: winter condensation damp in older stock

name: Aldridge
description: Aldridge is a prosperous suburban town in the eastern part of the borough, with a high proportion of detached and semi-detached owner-occupied properties. Residents tend to invest in property improvement and regular maintenance.
commonRepairNeeds: TV mounting and shelving in family rooms
seasonalIssues: summer pressure washing of block paving driveways

name: Brownhills
description: Brownhills sits at the northern edge of the West Midlands, bordering Staffordshire. It has a mix of older terraced housing and post-war social housing, with growing numbers of landlord-owned rental properties.
commonRepairNeeds: gutter cleaning on exposed northern-facing properties
seasonalIssues: wind exposure increases fence repair demand
gutter blockages from trees near Chasewater

name: Darlaston
description: Darlaston is a former industrial town south of Walsall with a mix of Victorian terraced housing and inter-war semi-detached properties. The area has a large rental market, making it popular for landlord maintenance services.
commonRepairNeeds: minor plumbing in older terraced properties
fence repairs on densely packed streets

name: Willenhall
description: Willenhall is a town in the south-western part of the borough, historically known for lock-making — fitting, as lock changes remain one of the most requested services here. The housing mix spans Victorian terraces to 1980s estates.
propertyTypes: modern town houses near Neachells Lane
seasonalIssues: cold winters increase lock mechanism failures

name: Wednesbury
description: Wednesbury is a historic market town south of Walsall with a strong mix of Victorian and inter-war housing, a growing student and young-professional rental market, and a high proportion of privately rented properties.
councilArea: Metropolitan Borough of Sandwell (adjacent to Walsall)

name: Pelsall
description: Pelsall is a quiet village-type settlement north of Walsall, bordering Brownhills and Rushall. Its predominantly owner-occupied housing stock — a mix of bungalows and detached homes — drives demand for home-improvement and regular maintenance.

name: Streetly
description: Streetly is an affluent suburb straddling the border between Walsall and Sutton Coldfield, with large detached homes and a homeowner demographic that invests heavily in interior improvements and quality finishes.
propertyTypes: large inter-war and post-war detached homes
seasonalIssues: winter gutter maintenance on large properties
councilArea: Metropolitan Borough of Walsall / Sutton Coldfield (boundary area)

name: Great Barr
description: Great Barr is a large suburban area on the southern edge of the Walsall borough, bordering Birmingham. It has a diverse housing mix from 1930s semis to 1960s estates, with a large owner-occupier and rental market.
seasonalIssues: spring external painting and fence treatment

name: Sutton Coldfield
description: Sutton Coldfield is a large, prosperous Royal Town northeast of Birmingham, bordering Walsall. Its wide range of high-value properties — from Victorian villas to executive new-builds near Mere Green — generates consistent demand for quality handyman and maintenance services.
propertyTypes: large Victorian and Edwardian detached homes
seasonalIssues: Sutton Park vicinity increases leaf-related gutter blockages
autumn fence repairs on large plots

name: Lichfield
description: Lichfield is a historic cathedral city in Staffordshire, just north of Walsall. Its mix of Georgian townhouses, Victorian semis, and modern suburban estates creates varied property maintenance needs, particularly for period properties.
commonRepairNeeds: period property repairs (sash windows, original joinery)
bathroom silicone and tiling in older properties
seasonalIssues: winter damp in Georgian period homes

name: Wolverhampton
description: Wolverhampton is a major city west of Walsall, with a large and diverse property market spanning city-centre flats, Victorian terraces, and suburban family homes. High rental density in many wards drives strong landlord service demand.
commonRepairNeeds: commercial property maintenance in city centre
seasonalIssues: winter damp in dense terraced streets

name: West Bromwich
description: West Bromwich is a large town southeast of Walsall with a dense Victorian terraced heritage and a high proportion of privately rented properties. Landlord maintenance services are in particularly high demand here.

name: Cannock
description: Cannock is a market town in south Staffordshire, north of Walsall, bordering Cannock Chase AONB. Its housing mix includes 1960s–90s suburban estates alongside newer commuter developments, with growing demand for home improvement services.
localLandmarks: Cannock Chase Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty
propertyTypes: some Victorian terraces near town centre
commonRepairNeeds: outdoor maintenance (decking, fencing) due to proximity to Chase
gutter cleaning on exposed northern plots
seasonalIssues: autumn leaf fall near Chase creates gutter issues
winter frost damage to exterior rendering

name: Tamworth
description: Tamworth is a historic market town and commuter settlement northeast of Birmingham, near the Walsall borough boundary. It has one of the largest shopping centres in the West Midlands and a growing new-build residential market.
commonRepairNeeds: TV mounting and shelving in new properties
seasonalIssues: autumn gutter clearing on new estates

name: Walsall Wood
description: Walsall Wood is a large village between Walsall and Brownhills, with predominantly inter-war and post-war housing. A quiet community with a high proportion of owner-occupied homes and growing demand for home improvement services.

name: Rushall
description: Rushall is a village suburb between Walsall and Aldridge, characterised by semi-rural surroundings and predominantly owner-occupied housing. Property sizes tend to be larger, driving demand for both interior and exterior maintenance.
seasonalIssues: autumn leaf management on larger gardens

name: Cheslyn Hay
description: Cheslyn Hay is a large village in Staffordshire, close to the Walsall border. Its mix of inter-war housing and 1980s–90s cul-de-sac estates makes it typical of outer West Midlands suburbia, with strong demand for general handyman services.
seasonalIssues: wind-exposed ridge location increases fence damage

name: Burntwood
description: Burntwood is a large town in Lichfield district, bordering the Walsall borough, with extensive post-war and 1980s housing estates. Its location between Walsall and Lichfield makes it a natural service area.
propertyTypes: post-war semi-detached council and owner-occupied stock
seasonalIssues: Staffordshire plateau wind increases fence storm damage
winter render maintenance on exposed properties
